content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-4444

January 9, 1978

John M. Witzel
Legislative Director
City of San Diego
1108 "O" Street, Room 204
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Witzel:

     Thank you for your letter of October 13, 1977, in which
you asked about the filing requirements for a lobbyist
employed by the City of San Diego. The lobbyist's salary
is paid by the League of California Cities which is reimbursed in full by San Diego. We also understand that the
lobbyist can only be hired and fired by the City and that
the League does not participate in such a decision.
Further, the League imposes no employment duties on the
lobbyist.

     You first ask whether the registration procedure for
this lobbyist is different from other lobbyists employed by
the City. The Political Reform Act requires a lobbyist to
register with the Secretary of State by filing a statement
which contains, among other items, the "name and business
address of each person by whom he is employed or with whom
he contracts for lobbying purposes..." (Government Code
Section 86101(b)). Although your lobbyist is paid by the
League, it is clear that the City is the actual employer
and should be so listed on the lobbyist's registration
statement. Similarly, the City should provide the written
authorization for the lobbyist.

     Therefore in answer to your first question, we do not
believe that the procedure for registration is different
for this particular lobbyist.

John M. Witzel
Page Two                                    January 9, 1978


     Your second question asks whether there is any specific
accounting procedures that will be required because of this
arrangement with the League. Because the salary of the
lobbyist is being paid by the League with reimbursement from
the City, we believe that the reports of the lobbyist and
the City should reflect this arrangement. Thus, the lobbyist
should report under the section Payments Received in Connection
with Lobbying Activity the "City of San Diego, salary trans-
mitted through accounts of the League of California
Cities." Similarly, the City's employer report should indi-
cate "the name of the lobbyist, salary transmitted through
the League of California Cities." We believe this reporting
accurately reflects the situation posed in your letter and
provides for clear and complete disclosure.

     Finally, you ask whether there is any responsibility
imposed on the League to report this contractual arrangement.
We believe that if the transaction is reported by the lobbyist
and the City as outlined above that the League does not have
any reporting responsibilities.

     Please call on me if you have any questions about this
letter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Robert M. Stern
                                        General Counsel

RMS:nc

THE CITY OF

SANDIEGO
1108"O" Street • Room 204 • Sacramento • California • 95814

October 13, 1977

DEPARTMENT OF
LEGISLATIVE
REPRESENTATION


Mr. Robert Stern
General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Comm.
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA. 95814

Dear Mr. Stern:

I would like to have advice from the Fair Political
Practices Commission regarding the possibility of
registering an employee as a lobbyist.

The City of San Diego has three registered lobbyists
in the Department of Legislative Representation who
are city employees. Our department has a Sacramento
office which is staffed full-time by a contractual
employee. The salary of this employee is paid by the
League of California Cities as is the office rent and
then the entire amount is reimbursed by the City of
San Diego and is reported as office overhead on the
"Prop. 9" reports. Although this employee is carried
on the League's health plan, that entire amount,
along with any other benefits such as workers' compen-
sation is reimbursed in full by San Diego. This employee
is therefore considered a contractual employee of the
City of San Diego.

Does the procedure to register this contractual employee
to lobby for the City differ from that of registered
city employees? Is there any specific accounting pro-
cedures that would be required other than those in
practice by the present city-employee lobbyist? Would
there by any responsibility upon the League of California
Cities to report because of the contractual arrangement?

Sincerely,

John M. Witzer
Legislative Director

JMW:nd

sections.question: Does the procedure to register this contractual employee to lobby for the City differ from that of registering city employees? Is there any specific accounting procedures that would be required other than those in practice by the present city-employee lobbyist? Would there by any responsibility upon the League of California Cities to report because of the contractual arrangement?
sections.question_synthetic: The City of San Diego seeks guidance on whether registering a contractual employee as a lobbyist follows the same procedures as registering city employees, and whether special accounting or reporting requirements apply given that the League of California Cities initially pays salary and benefits before reimbursement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or short answer section present. Document shows some OCR degradation/scanning artifacts but core content is clear. This is dated October 13, 1977, sent to FPPC requesting advice. The document ID (78ADV-78-077) suggests this may be part of a file where FPPC provided a response, but that response is not included in the provided text.

Is there any specific accounting procedures that would be required other than those in practice by the present city-employee lobbyist? Would there by any responsibility upon the League of California Cities to report because of the contractual arrangement?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the City of San Diego's Department of Legislative Representation asking the FPPC for advice on registering a contractual employee as a lobbyist and clarifying related accounting and reporting responsibilities.
